name: Science World, Vancouver
concept&appearance: Originally constructed as the Expo Centre for Expo 86, the building is an iconic geodesic dome designed by architect Bruno Freschi, inspired by Buckminster Fuller. Often nicknamed 'The Golf Ball,' the sphere is composed of 766 steel triangles and 391 lights, which can be programmed for dynamic lighting displays. The structure sits on a reinforced concrete deck supported by piles over the waters of False Creek, creating a striking waterfront landmark that dominates the local skyline.
permanent_exhibitions: 1. Eureka! Gallery: A high-energy hall focusing on physics, featuring interactive exhibits on water, light, sound, and motion (e.g., a water table, launch pads). 2. BodyWorks: Dedicated to human biology and anatomy, encouraging visitors to explore how the body functions through physical challenges and anatomical displays. 3. Search: Sara Stern Gallery: A nature-themed gallery showcasing the wonders of the natural world, including a real beaver lodge, a T. rex skeleton, and live animals like bees and insects. 4. Wonder Gallery: A specialized space for early learners (ages 0-5) to explore splashing, crawling, and building in a safe environment. 5. Tinkering Space: The WorkSafeBC Gallery: Focuses on engineering and problem-solving, allowing visitors to build, test, and fix creations. 6. Our World (BMO Sustainability Gallery): Explores sustainability, energy, and waste management. 7. Creative Technology Gallery: A newer exhibition co-presented by RBC and Beedie, featuring three zones (Dream, Build, Play) that blend science, art, and technology. 8. Ken Spencer Science Park: An outdoor exhibition park focusing on urban sustainability and the environment.
central_hall: The main interior hub centers around the 'Peter Brown Family Centre Stage,' a high-energy gathering space located on the first level. It features an open layout that connects the entrance to various galleries and includes a large stage area used for spectacular live science demonstrations that are visible from multiple vantage points.
special_effects_theaters: 1. OMNIMAX Theatre: Located at the top of the dome (Level 5), this theater features a massive 5-story high, 27-meter diameter domed screen and a 28-speaker sound system, making it one of the largest of its kind (currently undergoing renovations). 2. Science Theatre: A high-definition theater located on Level 2 used for screening educational films, 3D movies, and hosting live theatrical science performances.
science_popularization_activities: The museum hosts daily live science shows at the Centre Stage involving explosions and chemistry demos. It runs 'Science World After Dark' events for adults (19+) to explore science with food and drinks. Outreach programs include 'Scientists and Innovators in the Schools' (SIS), which brings scientists into classrooms across British Columbia, and 'Super Science Club' for after-school programs in underserved schools.
